title: Cynanchum proctorianum
taxonomy: Cynanchum proctorianum is a species of flowering plant in the dogbane family, Apocynaceae. The classification of Cynanchum proctorianum is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, and is further classified within the phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Gentianales, family Apocynaceae, and genus Cynanchum. This classification reflects its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Cynanchum proctorianum shares similarities with other dogbane species. Its classification helps to identify and understand the evolutionary history of this species.

intro: Cynanchum proctorianum, also known as Proctor's Trumpet Vine or Dragon's Milk Vine, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating respiratory problems. Cynanchum proctorianum is rich in bioactive constituents, including glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, such as proctorianum and cynanchnum, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cynanchum proctorianum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or extract, and is also used in traditional remedies, such as tinctures and salves, to promote overall well-being.
